Insert a record into a table. Returns the created record object keyed by column name (no envelope). Reserved tables (`reserved_*`) are system-managed and cannot be mutated through record write endpoints.

get.description: List table records with token pagination only. Use the datastore query endpoint for joins, aggregates, or custom read-only SQL.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Max number of rows to return.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Max number of rows to return.

parameters.name: filter

schema.description: Optional repeated JSON filters for advanced comparisons. Each `filter` value must be a JSON object with shape `{"field":"<column_name>","op":"<operator>","value":<comparison_value>}`. Allowed operators are: `eq`, `ne`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `like`, `ilike`. Repeat the query parameter to combine multiple filters with AND semantics. Examples: `filter={"field":"amount","op":"gt","value":100}` and `filter={"field":"status","op":"eq","value":"OPEN"}`.
schema.title: Filter

parameters.description: Optional repeated JSON filters for advanced comparisons. Each `filter` value must be a JSON object with shape `{"field":"<column_name>","op":"<operator>","value":<comparison_value>}`. Allowed operators are: `eq`, `ne`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `like`, `ilike`. Repeat the query parameter to combine multiple filters with AND semantics. Examples: `filter={"field":"amount","op":"gt","value":100}` and `filter={"field":"status","op":"eq","value":"OPEN"}`.

parameters.name: sort

schema.description: Optional repeated JSON sort clauses. Each `sort` value must be a JSON object with shape `{"field":"<column_name>","direction":"<direction>"}`. Allowed directions are: `asc`, `desc`. Repeat the query parameter to provide multi-column sorting in priority order. Example: `sort={"field":"created_at","direction":"desc"}`.
schema.title: Sort

parameters.description: Optional repeated JSON sort clauses. Each `sort` value must be a JSON object with shape `{"field":"<column_name>","direction":"<direction>"}`. Allowed directions are: `asc`, `desc`. Repeat the query parameter to provide multi-column sorting in priority order. Example: `sort={"field":"created_at","direction":"desc"}`.

parameters.name: page_token

schema.description: Opaque token from a previous response page.
schema.title: Page Token

parameters.description: Opaque token from a previous response page.

parameters.name: mode

schema.description: Row-visibility mode for RLS-enabled tables. Omitted/`USER` (default) scopes rows to the signed-in user's own records — the per-user semantics an app app expects. `ADMIN` returns/operates on every member's rows and requires permission to administer the table; a caller without it gets a 403. Ignored for non-RLS tables, whose rows are shared by all members.
schema.title: Mode

parameters.description: Row-visibility mode for RLS-enabled tables. Omitted/`USER` (default) scopes rows to the signed-in user's own records — the per-user semantics an app app expects. `ADMIN` returns/operates on every member's rows and requires permission to administer the table; a caller without it gets a 403. Ignored for non-RLS tables, whose rows are shared by all members.

get.description: Fetch one record by primary key value (returns the record object, no envelope). The `record_id` path segment is the table's primary key value as stored in the table, not necessarily a UUID.

parameters.name: mode

schema.description: Row-visibility mode for RLS-enabled tables. Omitted/`USER` (default) scopes rows to the signed-in user's own records — the per-user semantics an app app expects. `ADMIN` returns/operates on every member's rows and requires permission to administer the table; a caller without it gets a 403. Ignored for non-RLS tables, whose rows are shared by all members.

parameters.description: Row-visibility mode for RLS-enabled tables. Omitted/`USER` (default) scopes rows to the signed-in user's own records — the per-user semantics an app app expects. `ADMIN` returns/operates on every member's rows and requires permission to administer the table; a caller without it gets a 403. Ignored for non-RLS tables, whose rows are shared by all members.

schema.description: Row-visibility mode for RLS-enabled tables. parameters.name: mode

schema.description: Row-visibility mode for RLS-enabled tables. Omitted/`USER` (default) scopes rows to the signed-in user's own records — the per-user semantics an app app expects. `ADMIN` returns/operates on every member's rows and requires permission to administer the table; a caller without it gets a 403. Ignored for non-RLS tables, whose rows are shared by all members.
schema.title: Mode

parameters.description: Row-visibility mode for RLS-enabled tables. Omitted/`USER` (default) scopes rows to the signed-in user's own records — the per-user semantics an app app expects. `ADMIN` returns/operates on every member's rows and requires permission to administer the table; a caller without it gets a 403. Ignored for non-RLS tables, whose rows are shared by all members.

post.description: Delete multiple records by primary key values. Returns the affected-row count.

parameters.name: mode

schema.description: Row-visibility mode for RLS-enabled tables. Omitted/`USER` (default) scopes rows to the signed-in user's own records — the per-user semantics an app app expects. `ADMIN` returns/operates on every member's rows and requires permission to administer the table; a caller without it gets a 403. Ignored for non-RLS tables, whose rows are shared by all members.
schema.title: Mode

parameters.description: Row-visibility mode for RLS-enabled tables. Omitted/`USER` (default) scopes rows to the signed-in user's own records — the per-user semantics an app app expects. `ADMIN` returns/operates on every member's rows and requires permission to administer the table; a caller without it gets a 403. Ignored for non-RLS tables, whose rows are shared by all members.

post.description: Execute a read-only SQL query inside the datastore schema. Joins, aggregates, subqueries, and cross-table reads are allowed, including across RLS-enabled tables — rows of RLS tables are scoped to the caller by default (pod admins included). Pass `mode=admin` to read every member's rows, which requires permission to administer each referenced RLS table. Only a single read-only statement is permitted; mutating statements and cross-schema references are rejected.

parameters.name: mode

schema.description: Row-visibility mode for RLS-enabled tables referenced by the query. Omitted/`USER` (default) scopes their rows to the signed-in user — the per-user data apps and functions expect. `ADMIN` returns every member's rows and requires permission to administer every RLS table the query touches; a caller without it gets a 403. Non-RLS tables are unaffected.
schema.title: Mode

parameters.description: Row-visibility mode for RLS-enabled tables referenced by the query. Omitted/`USER` (default) scopes their rows to the signed-in user — the per-user data apps and functions expect. `ADMIN` returns every member's rows and requires permission to administer every RLS table the query touches; a caller without it gets a 403. Non-RLS tables are unaffected.

Get the skill guide markdown for a connector. Pass `provider=lemma` or `provider=composio` to get provider-specific instructions when the app supports both. Falls back to the generic doc if no provider-specific file exists. Returns 404 if no skill doc has been generated yet.
Get a specific connector by ID along with its operation catalog

post.description: Create a pod-owned agent definition with runtime, toolsets, and schemas.

get.description: List pod-owned agent definitions visible to the current user.
Get one pod-owned agent definition by its stable name.

patch.description: Update an agent definition, including prompt instruction, runtime, toolsets, and schemas.

delete.description: Delete a pod-owned agent definition by name.

get.description: Get explicit resource grants assigned to an agent.

put.description: Replace explicit resource grants assigned to an agent.

post.description: Run a raw web search and return structured results.
Record a maintainer-facing feedback report about system issues, skill issues, incorrect knowledge, or other unexpected behavior.

Create a new pod-scoped conversation. When agent_name is omitted, the conversation uses the default pod assistant. Workflow and sub-agent executions also use conversations as their external execution handle.

get.description: List root conversations for the current user in a pod. Use agent_name to list conversations for a specific pod agent; omit it to list default pod assistant conversations. Child (sub-agent) conversations are omitted by default; pass parent_id to list the children of a specific conversation instead.
Get a single pod-scoped assistant or agent conversation by id.

patch.description: Update mutable conversation settings for a pod-scoped conversation. The conversation runtime is used by future runs; message sends do not carry per-request runtime overrides.

get.description: List the latest persisted messages in chronological order. Pass next_page_token as page_token to fetch the next older page above the current page.

post.description: Append a user message to a pod-scoped conversation and stream runtime events over Server-Sent Events until the active run completes. User messages can also be appended while a run is already active; the next harness step sees the new message in persisted history.

get.description: List pending user-interaction tool calls (request_approval and ask_user) awaiting the user in a conversation.

post.description: Record the user's decision/answers for a paused request_approval or ask_user call and start a fresh run that resumes the agent. For an approved request_approval the wrapped tool runs as the user; the response body carries ask_user answers under `response.answers`.

get.description: Subscribe to Server-Sent Events for an existing pod-scoped conversation. The stream closes immediately when the conversation has no active run. Optionally filter to a specific internal run id for reconnects.

post.description: Request cancellation of the active internal run for a conversation.

Mint a short-lived, signed embed URL for a widget the caller may view.

Per-view (not baked into the persisted tool result) so the token stays
ephemeral and membership is re-checked each time the widget is opened.
Create a new function in a pod. Do not send input_schema, output_schema, or config_schema; the platform derives those schemas from the function code and returns them in the response.

get.description: List all functions in a pod
Get a function by name

patch.description: Update a function. When code is supplied, the platform re-derives the function input_schema and output_schema and returns the refreshed function.

get.description: Get explicit resource grants assigned to a function.

put.description: Replace explicit resource grants assigned to a function.

post.description: Execute a function

get.description: List runs for a function
Get a specific function run

post.description:
Promote a conversation widget into a persisted app.

The widget and the app are the same artifact at two lifecycle stages: this
fetches the widget's stored HTML and deploys it as the app's bundle —
identical to what was shown.

Create a workflow definition. The graph (`nodes`/`edges`) can be included in this call to create a ready-to-run workflow in one step, or omitted to create a shell and upload the graph later with `workflow.graph.update`.

get.description: List all workflows in a pod.
Get a single workflow definition including graph and start configuration.

patch.description: Update workflow-level metadata such as description and schedule mode. Workflow names are immutable after creation. Use `workflow.graph.update` for nodes and edges.

put.description: Replace the workflow graph. Agent/function node `input_mapping` entries must use explicit typed bindings. Use `{type: "expression", value: "start.payload.issue.key"}` for context lookups and `{type: "literal", value: "abc"}` for fixed JSON values.

post.description: Create a new run for this workflow. Takes no request body: if the workflow's entry node is a FORM node the run is created WAITING on it (see `active_wait` in the response) and input is submitted via `workflow.run.form.submit`; otherwise the run executes immediately. Trigger payloads for scheduled/event/datastore starts are supplied by the platform, not through this endpoint.

get.description: List recent runs for a given workflow.
Render an HTML visualization for debugging workflow graph structure.

post.description: Submit the form the run is waiting on. `node_id` must match the run's active HUMAN wait (409 when the run is not waiting on a form, 422 on node mismatch, 403 when the wait is assigned to someone else). The submitted `inputs` become the form node's output, available to later nodes as `<node_id>.<field>`.
Cancel a non-terminal run. The active wait (if any) is cancelled in the same transaction; late completion events for cancelled waits are dropped. Cancelling a terminal run returns 409.

get.summary: List Workflow Runs Waiting For Current User
get.description: The current user's approval queue: active form waits assigned to them, with the owning run.
Get current state, context, step history, and the active wait (when WAITING) of a workflow run.
Render an HTML view of a run overlaid on its workflow graph.

put.description:
Create the surface for a platform, or merge updates into the existing one.

A surface is unique per ``pod_id + platform``, so this single idempotent
write covers create, config edits, channel routing, account/credential
changes, and enable/disable. Only fields present in the request are applied
on update.

get.description:
Everything needed to finish setting up this platform's surface.

Merges the static platform checklist with live webhook + admin-consent
state. Works before the surface exists (guide only) and after (live state).

List the channels/groups this surface bot can be configured to respond in.

Returns an empty list for platforms without an enumerable channel concept
(Telegram groups, WhatsApp, email).

post.description: Handle platform-level webhook callbacks.

get.summary: Verify surface webhook using the platform callback URL
get.description: Webhook verification endpoint for platforms that require it.

post.description: Handle webhooks addressed to one concrete surface.

get.summary: Verify surface webhook using a surface-level callback URL
get.description: Webhook verification endpoint for platforms that require it.

post.description: Upload an image asset and receive a public icon URL.

get.description: Fetch a previously uploaded public icon asset.
List available subscription plans.
Get the current user's personal subscription.

post.description: Start a new subscription.

get.description: Get subscription status.
Get subscription for an organization.

post.description: Start monthly-invoiced team billing for an organization.
Cancel an organization's team billing subscription.

get.description: List invoices for an organization.
Get or create the current unpaid monthly invoice.

post.description: Create a hosted checkout URL for an unpaid invoice.
Cancel subscription.

get.description: Get seat availability information.

post.description: Handle webhooks from DodoPayments.

AccountCreateSchema.title: AccountCreateSchema

column.description: Column definition to append to the table. Existing column names cannot be reused.

AddColumnRequest.description: Schema for adding a column to a table.

AgentNode.title: AgentNode
AgentNode.description: Agent node. The run waits on the agent conversation to complete.

input_mapping.title: Input Mapping
input_mapping.description: Explicit mapping from agent input key to either an expression or a literal JSON value. Strings are never auto-interpreted.

AgentRunApprovalDecision.title: AgentRunApprovalDecision
AgentRunApprovalDecision.description: User decision scope for a run approval.

AgentRunStatus.title: AgentRunStatus
AgentRunStatus.description: Internal lifecycle for one harness execution.

AgentRuntimeConfig.title: AgentRuntimeConfig
AgentRuntimeConfig.description: Agent runtime selector using a profile plus optional catalog model.

AgentSummaryResponse.title: AgentSummaryResponse
AgentSummaryResponse.description:
Lean agent shape for list responses.

Omits the heavy single-resource fields (`instruction`, `input_schema`,
`output_schema`, `agent_runtime`) — fetch those from `agent.get`. Keeps
`toolsets` so list cards can show a connection count.

AgentToolset.title: AgentToolset
AgentToolset.description: Known tool bundles an agent may request.

ApiKeyCredentialsResponseSchema.title: ApiKeyCredentialsResponseSchema
ApiKeyCredentialsResponseSchema.description: Schema for API key credentials response.

AppTriggerSummaryResponseSchema.title: AppTriggerSummaryResponseSchema
AppTriggerSummaryResponseSchema.description:
Lean trigger shape for list responses.

Omits the heavy `config_schema` / `payload_schema` / `payload_example` JSON
blobs — fetch those from `connector.trigger.get`.

AvailableSurfaceChannelResponse.title: AvailableSurfaceChannelResponse
AvailableSurfaceChannelResponse.description: A channel/group the surface bot can be configured to respond in.

BillingHistoryResponse.title: BillingHistoryResponse
BillingHistoryResponse.description: Response schema for billing invoice history.

BillingInvoiceResponse.title: BillingInvoiceResponse
BillingInvoiceResponse.description: Response schema for a billing invoice.

records.title: Records
records.description: List of record payload objects to insert.

upsert.title: Upsert
upsert.description: When true, insert records and update existing rows that conflict on the table primary key.

records.title: Records
records.description: List of record updates. Each item must include the table primary key field.

required.title: Required
required.description: Whether the column is required (NOT NULL)

unique.title: Unique
unique.description: Whether the column must have unique values

system.title: System
system.description: Whether the column is system-managed by the backend.

computed.title: Computed
computed.description: Whether this is a computed column

ColumnSchema.title: ColumnSchema
ColumnSchema.description: Schema for a datastore table column.

ConnectRequestInitiateSchema.title: ConnectRequestInitiateSchema
ConnectRequestInitiateSchema.description: Schema for initiating a connect request.

ConnectorDetailResponseSchema.title: ConnectorDetailResponseSchema
ConnectorDetailResponseSchema.description: Schema for connector details including operation catalog.

permissions.description: Optional resource grants to apply to the new agent in the same request. Equivalent to calling the permissions-replace endpoint right after create — grants are keyed by resource_name.

CreateAppFromWidgetRequest.title: CreateAppFromWidgetRequest
CreateAppFromWidgetRequest.description:
Promote a conversation widget into a persisted app.

The widget's stored HTML (addressed by conversation + tool call) is wrapped
into a standalone document and deployed as the app's bundle — the artifact
is identical to what the widget showed. See docs/app-widget-unification.md.

code.title: Code
code.description: Python source for the function. When provided, the platform analyzes the code and populates input_schema, output_schema, and config_schema on the returned function.

CreateFunctionRequest.title: CreateFunctionRequest
CreateFunctionRequest.description:
Request to create a function.

Input and output schemas are derived from the submitted code and returned
on the function response. They are not accepted in create requests.

CreateInvoicePaymentUrlRequest.title: CreateInvoicePaymentUrlRequest
CreateInvoicePaymentUrlRequest.description: Request to generate a payment URL for an invoice.

CreateInvoicePaymentUrlResponse.title: CreateInvoicePaymentUrlResponse
CreateInvoicePaymentUrlResponse.description: Response with invoice payment checkout URL.

data.title: Data
data.description: Record object keyed by table column names.

name.title: Name
name.description: Stable pod-scoped schedule name used for import/export upserts.

account_id.title: Account Id
account_id.description: Connected connector account used to provision provider-backed webhook schedules.

connector_trigger_id.title: Connector Trigger Id
connector_trigger_id.description: Connector trigger id for agent WEBHOOK schedules. Do not provide this for workflow schedules; workflow WEBHOOK schedules derive it from the workflow start configuration.

filter_instruction.title: Filter Instruction
filter_instruction.description: Optional schedule-level LLM filter instruction. Filters belong to the schedule, not the workflow start.

filter_output_schema.title: Filter Output Schema
filter_output_schema.description: Optional schema for the schedule-level filter output. Filters belong to the schedule, not the workflow start.

CreateScheduleRequest.title: CreateScheduleRequest
CreateScheduleRequest.description: Request to create a pod schedule.

name.title: Name
name.description: Table name. Use alphanumeric and underscore only. Names prefixed with `reserved_` are system-managed and should not be user-created.

primary_key_column.title: Primary Key Column
primary_key_column.description: Primary key column name. If not `id`, it must also be declared in `columns`.

columns.title: Columns
columns.description: Table column definitions. Each column name must be unique. Use `type`, `required`, `default`, `foreign_key`, and `computed` as needed. The backend also materializes physical system columns so table metadata reflects the real schema: `id` when omitted as the primary key, `created_at`, `updated_at`, and `user_id` when RLS is enabled.

config.title: Config
config.description: Optional table metadata/configuration. This updates table config metadata and does not directly alter physical columns.

enable_rls.title: Enable Rls
enable_rls.description: Enable row-level security for this table. When enabled, API reads/writes are scoped by current user.

CreateTableRequest.title: CreateTableRequest
CreateTableRequest.description: Schema for creating a new table.

table_name.title: Table Name
table_name.description: Table name inside the datastore to subscribe to.

operations.title: Operations
operations.description: Datastore operations that should trigger this flow. One or more of INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE.

table_name.title: Table Name
table_name.description: Table name inside the datastore to subscribe to.

operations.title: Operations
operations.description: Datastore operations that should trigger this flow. One or more of INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE.

config.description: Datastore trigger configuration for this workflow.
Datastore trigger configuration for this workflow.

DatastoreCountResponse.title: DatastoreCountResponse
DatastoreCountResponse.description: Schema for bulk mutation responses reporting an affected-row count.

DatastoreDataType.title: DatastoreDataType
DatastoreDataType.description: Supported data types for datastore columns.

query.title: Query
query.description: Read-only SQL query executed inside this datastore schema. A single SELECT statement only; mutating statements (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, ALTER, DROP, CREATE, TRUNCATE, ...) and cross-schema references are rejected. Joins, aggregates, and subqueries across tables are allowed, including RLS-enabled tables — rows of an RLS table are scoped to the caller unless they administer it. Example: `SELECT id, amount FROM expenses WHERE amount > 100 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20`.

DatastoreQueryRequest.title: DatastoreQueryRequest
DatastoreQueryRequest.description: Schema for executing read-only SQL within a datastore.

DatastoreQueryResponse.title: DatastoreQueryResponse
DatastoreQueryResponse.description: Schema for read-only datastore query results.

DecisionNode.title: DecisionNode
DecisionNode.description:
Decision node. Routes to the first rule whose condition is truthy;
falls through to the default outgoing edge when no rule matches.

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: JMESPath condition evaluated against the run context. The first rule whose condition is truthy selects the next node. Example: `collect_input.decision == 'approved'`.

connector_trigger_id.title: Connector Trigger Id
connector_trigger_id.description: Connector trigger identifier to subscribe to.

connector_trigger_id.title: Connector Trigger Id
connector_trigger_id.description: Connector trigger identifier to subscribe to.

config.description: Connector trigger configuration for this workflow.
Connector trigger configuration for this workflow.

value.title: Value
value.description: JMESPath expression evaluated against the run context. Example: `start.payload.issue.key` or `collect_input.amount`. Expressions that resolve to nothing fail the run unless `optional` is set.

optional.title: Optional
optional.description: When true, an expression that resolves to nothing yields null instead of failing the run.

ExpressionInputBinding.title: ExpressionInputBinding
ExpressionInputBinding.description: Resolve a value from the run context using a JMESPath expression.

FileChildSchema.title: FileChildSchema
FileChildSchema.description:
A derived child artifact of a processed document (converted markdown,
an extracted figure, or a renderable page). Fetch its bytes from
``GET …/files/children/content?path=<child path>``.

scope_path.title: Scope Path
scope_path.description: Optional folder path to scope search results.

FileSummaryResponse.title: FileSummaryResponse
FileSummaryResponse.description:
Lean file/folder shape for list responses.

Omits the heavy `metadata` (JSONB) and `last_processing_error` (can be
multi-KB) — fetch those from `file.get`. Keeps `description` (small, may be
shown in list views).

FlowRunStatus.title: FlowRunStatus
FlowRunStatus.description:
Status of a flow run.

PENDING exists only in memory before the first advance; persisted runs
are RUNNING, WAITING, or terminal. WAITING is reserved for human form
waits. Runs suspended on platform work such as an agent, function job, or
timer remain RUNNING; the active wait row records the exact wait_type.

FlowSummaryResponse.title: FlowSummaryResponse
FlowSummaryResponse.description:
Lean workflow shape for list responses.

Omits the full graph (`nodes`/`edges`/`start`) — fetch those from
`workflow.get`. Carries cheap derived `node_count`/`node_types` so list
views can show step counts and participant badges without the graph.

ForeignKeySpec.title: ForeignKeySpec
ForeignKeySpec.description: Specification for a foreign key constraint.

FormNode.title: FormNode
FormNode.description:
Form node for user input. The run waits on it until the form is
submitted via the form-submit endpoint.

assignee_pod_member_id.title: Assignee Pod Member Id
assignee_pod_member_id.description: Pod member assigned to submit this form.

assignee_pod_member_id_expression.title: Assignee Pod Member Id Expression
assignee_pod_member_id_expression.description: Optional JMESPath expression resolving to a pod member id. Takes precedence over assignee_pod_member_id.

input_schema.title: Input Schema
input_schema.description: Input JSON schema derived from the function code.

output_schema.title: Output Schema
output_schema.description: Output JSON schema derived from the function code.

config_schema.title: Config Schema
config_schema.description: Optional configuration schema derived from the function code.

python_packages.title: Python Packages
python_packages.description: pip dependencies declared in the code's #python_packages header.

input_schema.title: Input Schema
input_schema.description: Input JSON schema derived from the function code.

output_schema.title: Output Schema
output_schema.description: Output JSON schema derived from the function code.

config_schema.title: Config Schema
config_schema.description: Optional configuration schema derived from the function code.

python_packages.title: Python Packages
python_packages.description: pip dependencies declared in the code's #python_packages header.

FunctionNode.title: FunctionNode
FunctionNode.description:
Function node. Completes inline for synchronous functions or waits on
the function run for asynchronous ones.

input_mapping.title: Input Mapping
input_mapping.description: Explicit mapping from function argument key to either an expression or a literal JSON value. Strings are never auto-interpreted.

FunctionRunSummaryResponse.title: FunctionRunSummaryResponse
FunctionRunSummaryResponse.description: Function run summary for list responses.

FunctionSummaryResponse.title: FunctionSummaryResponse
FunctionSummaryResponse.description:
Lean function shape for list responses.

Omits the heavy `input_schema` / `output_schema` / `config_schema` (full JSON
schemas derived from the function code) and `code` — fetch those from
`function.get`.

HarnessKind.title: HarnessKind
HarnessKind.description: Runtime framework used to execute an agent.

value.title: Value
value.description: Literal JSON value forwarded exactly as provided. Use this for strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, or objects that should not be interpreted as JMESPath expressions.

LiteralInputBinding.title: LiteralInputBinding
LiteralInputBinding.description: Pass a literal JSON value into the target input without resolution.

LoopNode.title: LoopNode
LoopNode.description:
Loop node. Iterates the body chain once per item; the aggregated
output is `{results: [...], count: n}` under the loop node id.

items_path.title: Items Path
items_path.description: JMESPath to an array in the run context to iterate over.

item_var_name.title: Item Var Name
item_var_name.description: Alias for the current item inside the loop body, available as `loop.<item_var_name>` (the item is always available as `loop.item`).

child_node_id.title: Child Node Id
child_node_id.description: Id of the first node of the loop body executed per item.

type.title: Type
type.description: Manual workflow start with no configuration payload.

type.title: Type
type.description: Manual workflow start with no configuration payload.

MessageKind.title: MessageKind
MessageKind.description:
Discriminates the flat message body.

A message carries exactly one kind. Textual kinds use ``text``; tool kinds
use ``tool_name``/``tool_call_id`` plus ``tool_args`` (call) or
``tool_result`` (return). There is no nested ``content`` object.

OperationDetail.title: OperationDetail
OperationDetail.description: Full operation metadata including input and output schemas.

operation_names.title: Operation Names
operation_names.description: Operation names to fetch. Omit or pass an empty list to return details for every operation in the connector.

OperationDetailsBatchRequest.title: OperationDetailsBatchRequest
OperationDetailsBatchRequest.description: Request multiple operation details in a single call.

items.title: Items
items.description: Operation details for the requested operations.

returned_count.title: Returned Count
returned_count.description: Number of operation details returned in this response.

OperationDetailsBatchResponse.title: OperationDetailsBatchResponse
OperationDetailsBatchResponse.description: Batch response containing full metadata for multiple operations.

query.title: Query
query.description: Optional discovery query used to rank or filter operations.

total_operations.title: Total Operations
total_operations.description: Total operations available for the connector.

returned_count.title: Returned Count
returned_count.description: Number of operations returned in this response.

OperationDiscoverResponse.title: OperationDiscoverResponse
OperationDiscoverResponse.description: Structured result for operation discovery within one connector.

relevance_score.title: Relevance Score
relevance_score.description: Relative relevance for the discovery query, from 0 to 1.

OperationSummary.title: OperationSummary
OperationSummary.description: Compact operation metadata for discovery flows.

OrganizationInvitationListResponse.title: OrganizationInvitationListResponse
OrganizationInvitationListResponse.description: Organization invitation list response with pagination.

OrganizationJoinPolicy.title: OrganizationJoinPolicy
OrganizationJoinPolicy.description: Who may self-join an organization, ordered from closed to open.

OrganizationMemberListResponse.title: OrganizationMemberListResponse
OrganizationMemberListResponse.description: Organization member list response with pagination.

PlanListResponse.title: PlanListResponse
PlanListResponse.description: Response schema for list of plans.

PodJoinPolicy.title: PodJoinPolicy
PodJoinPolicy.description: Who may self-join a pod, ordered from closed to open.

PodMemberUpdateRoleRequest.title: PodMemberUpdateRoleRequest
PodMemberUpdateRoleRequest.description: Pod member role update request schema.

PodRole.title: PodRole
PodRole.description: Explicit pod role names used across authorization and pod APIs.

RecordAccessMode.title: RecordAccessMode
RecordAccessMode.description:
Row-visibility mode for record reads/writes on RLS-enabled tables.

``USER`` (the default when the ``mode`` param is omitted) scopes rows to the
caller's own ``user_id`` so app apps keep their per-user semantics — pod
admins included. ``ADMIN`` returns and operates on every member's rows and
requires the caller to administer the table (otherwise the request is
rejected). The mode is a no-op for non-RLS tables, whose rows are shared by
every member regardless.

Endpoints expose this as an *optional* query param (default ``None`` ==
``USER``) rather than one defaulting to ``RecordAccessMode.USER``: a named
enum query param carrying a literal default makes the TypeScript generator
emit ``mode: RecordAccessMode = 'USER'``, which fails strict typecheck (a raw
string is not assignable to the enum). An optional param generates the valid
``mode?: RecordAccessMode`` while keeping one shared, CAPS-valued enum type.

category.description: High-level category for the feedback report.

subject.title: Subject
subject.description: Short subject line summarizing the report.

issue_encountered.title: Issue Encountered
issue_encountered.description: What issue, problem, or incorrect information was encountered.

expected_behavior.title: Expected Behavior
expected_behavior.description: What the caller expected to happen instead.

suggested_next_steps.title: Suggested Next Steps
suggested_next_steps.description: Optional proposed fixes, follow-ups, or next steps.

ReportFeedbackRequest.title: ReportFeedbackRequest
ReportFeedbackRequest.description: Request payload for maintainer feedback reports.

success.title: Success
success.description: Whether the feedback was recorded successfully.

feedback_id.title: Feedback Id
feedback_id.description: Identifier of the created feedback report.

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: Authenticated user associated with the report.

agent_id.title: Agent Id
agent_id.description: Delegated agent associated with the report, if available.

ReportFeedbackResponse.title: ReportFeedbackResponse
ReportFeedbackResponse.description: Response payload for maintainer feedback reports.

ScheduleFireStatus.title: ScheduleFireStatus
ScheduleFireStatus.description: Outcome of the most recent fire attempt, recorded for debuggability.

schedule_type.description: Time trigger mode for this workflow definition. Concrete schedule values are provided by pod schedules.
Time trigger mode for this workflow definition. Concrete schedule values are provided by pod schedules.

success_url.title: Success Url
success_url.description: URL to redirect after successful payment

cancel_url.title: Cancel Url
cancel_url.description: URL to redirect if payment is cancelled

message.title: Message
message.default: Please complete payment at the provided URL

StartTeamBillingRequest.title: StartTeamBillingRequest
StartTeamBillingRequest.description: Request to start monthly-invoiced team billing.

SurfaceAdminConsentInfo.title: SurfaceAdminConsentInfo
SurfaceAdminConsentInfo.description: Admin-consent state for surfaces that require an OAuth grant (Teams).

SurfaceConfigResponse.title: SurfaceConfigResponse
SurfaceConfigResponse.description: Mirrors SurfaceBehaviorConfigInput: what you send is what you get back.

SurfaceSetupAction.title: SurfaceSetupAction
SurfaceSetupAction.description:
A concrete thing the user must do to finish wiring up a surface.

Only emitted when the user actually has to act (custom/bring-your-own-app
credentials, or a pending OAuth grant). Each action carries where to go
(``link``), ordered ``steps``, and the values to paste (``fields``).

SurfaceSetupActionField.title: SurfaceSetupActionField
SurfaceSetupActionField.description: A copy-able value the user pastes into their provider dashboard.

SurfaceSetupResponse.title: SurfaceSetupResponse
SurfaceSetupResponse.description:
Everything a caller needs to finish setting up a surface, in one read.

Merges the former setup-status, admin-consent, and platform-checklist
endpoints. Works both before a surface exists (`exists=False`, guide only)
and after.

``ready`` is True when the user has nothing left to do (system credentials,
or an already-granted consent). ``actions`` is populated *only* when the
user must act — e.g. point their own Slack/Teams/WhatsApp app at Lemma —
so the UI can show a clean "Ready" state otherwise.

SurfaceUpsertRequest.title: SurfaceUpsertRequest
SurfaceUpsertRequest.description:
The single create-or-update body for `PUT /surfaces/{platform}`.

A surface is uniquely identified by `pod_id + platform`, so this one
request handles both creation and partial update. Only the fields present
in the request are applied on update (merge semantics); `is_enabled`
defaults to True on create and is only changed on update when sent.

TableSummaryResponse.title: TableSummaryResponse
TableSummaryResponse.description:
Lean table shape for list responses.

Omits the full `columns` definitions and `config` — fetch those from
`table.get`. Exposes a cheap `column_count` for list views.

code.title: Code
code.description: Updated Python source for the function. When provided, the platform re-analyzes the code and refreshes input_schema, output_schema, and config_schema on the returned function.

data.title: Data
data.description: Partial record patch keyed by table column names.

config.title: Config
config.description: Replacement metadata/config payload for the table.

enable_rls.title: Enable Rls
enable_rls.description: Toggle per-user row-level security. Only allowed on an empty table: enabling adds the user_id ownership column and isolation policy, disabling removes the policy. Omit to leave RLS unchanged.

WaitUntilNode.title: WaitUntilNode
WaitUntilNode.description: Wait node. Suspends the run until the scheduler wakes it.

max_results.title: Max Results
max_results.description: Maximum number of search results to return

WebSearchRequest.title: WebSearchRequest
WebSearchRequest.description: Request model for standard web search

error.title: Error
error.description: Error message if the search was not successful

WebSearchResponse.title: WebSearchResponse
WebSearchResponse.description: Response model for standard web search

icon_url.title: Icon Url
icon_url.description: Optional public icon URL for the workflow.

start.title: Start
start.description: Start configuration. If omitted, the workflow can be started manually via `workflow.start`.

mode.description: Workflow schedule ownership mode. `GLOBAL` means one pod-level workflow schedule is allowed; `USER` is reserved for per-user schedule ownership.

nodes.title: Nodes
nodes.description: Optional initial graph nodes. When provided, the graph is stored at creation time so a separate `workflow.graph.update` call is not required. Omit (or pass an empty list) to create a shell and upload the graph later. Node `input_mapping` entries must use explicit typed bindings like `{"type": "expression", "value": "start.payload.x"}`.

edges.title: Edges
edges.description: Optional initial graph edges connecting the provided nodes.

WorkflowEdge.title: WorkflowEdge
WorkflowEdge.description: An edge in the workflow graph.

nodes.title: Nodes
nodes.description: Complete node list for the workflow graph. Agent/function `input_mapping` entries must use explicit typed bindings like `{"type": "expression", "value": "start.payload.issue.key"}` or `{"type": "literal", "value": "finance"}`.

edges.title: Edges
edges.description: Complete edge list connecting the provided nodes.

start.title: Start
start.description: Optional replacement start configuration stored with the graph.

WorkflowGraphUpdateRequest.title: WorkflowGraphUpdateRequest
WorkflowGraphUpdateRequest.description: Named request body for replacing a workflow graph.

node_id.title: Node Id
node_id.description: Id of the FORM node being submitted. Must match the run's active wait; mismatches return 422.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Form field values keyed by field name.

WorkflowRunFormSubmitRequest.title: WorkflowRunFormSubmitRequest
WorkflowRunFormSubmitRequest.description: Canonical form submission payload — identical across web, SDKs, CLI.

WorkflowRunResponse.title: WorkflowRunResponse
WorkflowRunResponse.description:
Full run state. `execution_context` is the same flat view that
workflow expressions resolve against (`<node_id>.<field>`, `start.*`,
`loop.*`). `active_wait` is set when the run is suspended, including
WAITING form waits and RUNNING platform waits.

icon_url.title: Icon Url
icon_url.description: Updated public icon URL for the workflow.

ErrorResponse.description:
Unified error envelope returned by every error response.

All error responses (domain errors, HTTP exceptions, request validation
failures, and unexpected errors) share this shape. See
``app.core.api.exception_handlers``.
ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse
